description: Prevents child nodes from processing MIDI events

properties.note: UI-only layout hint. Does not affect processing.

gaps.question: Confirm that no_midi uses wrap::no_midi<T> which makes handleHiseEvent() a no-op. Audio processing is completely unaffected -- children process audio normally but receive zero MIDI events. Is this the only difference from a regular chain?
gaps.context: Users need to know that no_midi is purely about event blocking, not audio modification.

gaps.question: The phase3 doc highlights preventing oscillators from receiving MIDI frequency changes when used as LFOs. In a polyphonic synthesiser context where MIDI is enabled by default, confirm that no_midi is the correct way to shield nodes from MIDI events. Does it block ALL event types (note-on, note-off, CC, pitch wheel, aftertouch)?
gaps.context: The LFO-oscillator use case is important for documentation. Need to confirm comprehensive event blocking.

notes: No_midi wraps DynamicSerialProcessor in wrap::no_midi<T>. It is the inverse of midichain: where midichain enables MIDI processing, no_midi disables it. The phase3 doc has a good practical example (preventing oscillator frequency changes from MIDI in polyphonic contexts). Serial processing is identical to chain for audio.
